Story 10.2 : stratégie RSI multi-période + Heikin-Ashi + corrections code review

Implémentation de RsiHaStrategy (2 phases sans timeout) :
- Phase 1 : 3 RSI (périodes 3,5,7) simultanément en zone oversold/overbought
- Phase 2 : attente indéfinie de la confirmation Heikin-Ashi
- SL = min(low) des N dernières bougies (LONG) / max(high) (SHORT)
- Enregistrée sous la clé "rsi_ha" dans StrategyRegistry

Corrections code review :
- _candle_history bornée à max(max_period×10, sl_lookback) — évite O(n²) longue durée
- Reset _signal_direction et _computed_sl_price en entrée de Phase 1
- Test AC5 réel ajouté (test_rsi_ha_no_signal_partial_rsi_ac5, mock RSI(7))
- 14 tests passent, 859 total, 0 régression

commit.message:
Animate approval button slide-in from right on state transition

Frame-based animation for StreamDeck approval buttons (slots 0-3).
When state transitions to awaiting-approval: clear all keys to black,
pause briefly, then slide each button in from the right over ~500ms
with ease-out-cubic easing and a left-to-right stagger.

- Add slideInFrame(), blackSVG(), extractSvgContent() to layouts.ts
- Add animateApprovalSlideIn() method with AbortController for interruption
- Modify renderAll() to detect transition into awaiting-approval
- Use Promise.all() for parallel per-frame key updates
- Restore non-approval slots (4+) after animation completes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(scripts): psychological trigger sequencing framework

Adds a master persuasion order system to script generation based on
analysis of winning Final Expense CTV reference scripts.

- Augments triggers seed with masterOrder, section, isCore fields
  (persuasion sequence: Social Proof → Authority → ... → Autonomy)
- New trigger-sequencer service builds TriggerSequenceContext from
  campaign triggers, sorts by masterOrder, splits into hook/body/cta
  sections, and generates a structured LLM prompt block
- Script generator now accepts triggerSequence, replaces hardcoded
  hint with dynamic sequence text (falls back to DEFAULT_TRIGGER_HINT)
- Adds 90s CTA rules to system prompt: phone twice, "Write that down"
  after first, emotional resolve + urgency between, simplicity close
- Script action loads campaign triggers in parallel and passes
  triggerSequence to the generator

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Windows endpoint crash — install VC++ runtime, use Start-Process

Root cause: GCP/Azure Windows Server images lack vcruntime140.dll,
causing MSVC-built binaries to crash with STATUS_DLL_NOT_FOUND
(0xC0000135). Additionally, sc.exe create/start silently fails
because networker-endpoint is not a native Windows service.

Fixes:
- release.yml: static-link CRT on Windows (-C target-feature=+crt-static)
  so future binaries are self-contained
- install.sh: install VC++ Redistributable before running binary on all
  Windows deploy paths (GCP startup, Azure run-command, LAN SSH)
- Replace sc.exe/schtasks /Run with Start-Process -NoNewWindow for
  reliable process start; keep schtasks only for reboot persistence
- Add detailed diagnostics: ZIP/binary size, extracted file listing,
  stderr capture, process existence checks

Verified: GCP Windows endpoint at 35.196.203.124:8080 now healthy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix WebSocket startup delay and hanging connection timeout (#7)

- Remove premature resetWatchdog() call at plugin.start() before any
  socket exists; the watchdog (refreshRate+60s = ~2min) was the only
  thing killing a hanging initial connection, causing ~2min startup delay
- Move resetWatchdog() into the 'open' event handler so the watchdog
  only ticks after a connection is successfully established
- Add 15s connection timeout in startAisStream() to detect and kill
  sockets stuck in CONNECTING state, triggering normal reconnect logic
- Clear connectTimeout in both 'open' and 'close' handlers to avoid leaks

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bq9B7vGRkBdzJACvGJRBh8

commit.message:
fix: use mipsle (little-endian) instead of mips for Keenetic routers

Keenetic routers use MIPS little-endian processors, not big-endian.
The installer was downloading the wrong binary architecture.

Changes:
- GOARCH=mips -> GOARCH=mipsle in Makefile and workflows
- Binary renamed: xkeen-ui-keenetic-mips -> xkeen-ui-keenetic-mipsle
- setup.sh now correctly detects and returns "mipsle" for MIPS devices
- arch.go updated to return correct binary name

commit.message:
feat(attestation): implement get_difficulty_assessment with DifficultyPath link index

assess_difficulty now links the created DifficultyAssessment entry directly from
request_ref (ExternalHash) using a new DifficultyPath link type, consistent with
how governance indexes badges via StudyToBadge. No path intermediate needed since
request_ref is already a valid DHT base address.

get_difficulty_assessment replaces the stub with a real get_links query on
DifficultyPath, returning the most recently linked record (links.last(), matching
get_current_phase behaviour) or None if no assessment exists.

Test 5.3 updated: single scenario verifies before/after assess_difficulty, decodes
the returned entry to check request_ref bytes and code_volume, and confirms a
different unassessed request_ref still returns null. Combines two cases in one
conductor to avoid resource exhaustion between back-to-back test runs.

tests/README.md: 54 → 55 pass; get_difficulty_assessment moved to resolved table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

BentoCard.css:
- Replace flex: 1 0 0 + aspect-ratio with flex: 0 0 50% on both image and content
  for full/desktop variant — gives explicit 50/50 split regardless of content width
- Add box-sizing: border-box to content column so padding doesn't overflow 50% basis
- Remove aspect-ratio (height is correctly driven by the content column; img fills via object-fit)

ComposableLandingPage.stories.tsx:
- Wrap BentoBlock AnimateIn in width: 100% div so block background extends full page width
  (outer container uses alignItems: center which was constraining the unwrapped AnimateIn)

- Update all consumers: Canvas.stories.tsx, ComposableLandingPage.stories.tsx

commit.message:
Rebuild leaderboard from all saved results, not just current run

Previously the leaderboard only included models from the current session,
so running model B would overwrite model A's entry. Now all result files
under results/*/*.json are loaded before aggregating scores, giving a
cumulative leaderboard across all runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(beads): ignore br runtime directories in .beads/.gitignore

The br (beads_rust) tool creates two runtime directories inside .beads/:

  .br_history/   — stores command history for interactive br sessions,
                   analogous to shell history files; machine-specific and
                   session-ephemeral
  .br_recovery/  — stores crash recovery state; only relevant while a br
                   command is actively running; should never be committed

These are generated at runtime and vary per machine and session. Committing
them would create noisy, meaningless diffs and pollute the shared history
with local artefacts that have no value to other contributors.

Added to .beads/.gitignore alongside the existing exclusions for SQLite
database files, lock files, and last-touched timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add CUBLAS troubleshooting steps to README

Documents the three-step fix for CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE on
SageMaker/CUDA 12.9: unset LD_LIBRARY_PATH (confirmed working),
upgrade nvidia-cublas-cu12, or reinstall vLLM with cu129 wheels.
Also notes the enforce_eager=True caveat.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix entity definitions against reference, correct climate modes, and harden protocol layer

Entity definitions cross-checked against Beormund/esphome-samsung-nasa reference:
- Fix NASA mode values (Auto=0, Cool=1, Dry=2, Fan=3, Heat=4)
- Fix 0x4426 (now indoor_generated_power, W) and add 0x4427 (indoor_cumulative_energy, kWh)
- Remove wrong divisor/signed from 0x4284, 0x8238; fix accuracy on 0x8413
- Fix number ranges, add missing divisor/signed on temp FSVs, add device_class duration
- Fix select option labels and order; move 0x409B from select to switch
- Fix text sensor mappings for 0x8000, 0x8001; expand 0x8235 error codes
- Add device_class heat to booster_heater binary sensor

Protocol and reliability fixes:
- Always use int16_t for signed values (matches Samsung NASA convention)
- Fix Variable decode signed left-shift UB
- Skip packet_number 0 (fire-and-forget sentinel collision)
- Fix millis() wraparound in timeout, retry, and FSV startup delay checks
- Fix include path to use __file__ (works with GitHub external_components)
- Add duplicate device address validation
- Clear custom/standard fan mode properly on climate mode switch
- Remove unused dest param from send_read, dead code from nasa.py
- Reserve rx_buffer_, log queue drops, use LONG_MIN sentinel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix workflow artifact passing between collect and report jobs

The generate-report workflow failed because it tried to download an
artifact named intel-db-{current_run_number}, which never exists since
the collect workflow uploaded with a different run number. Fixed by:

- Using a fixed artifact name "intel-db" with overwrite:true
- Switching to dawidd6/action-download-artifact@v6 for cross-workflow
  artifact downloads
- Adding previous DB download to collect workflow for data continuity
- Adding report type auto-detection from cron schedule
- Adding error handling in report generation
- Fixing requirements.txt path (was backend/requirements.txt)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
